name: burgess_principle_check
description: Apply the Burgess Principle binary test to a decision or action that affects an individual. The test asks: 'Was the individual considered as an individual human being, or were they processed as a unit within a system?' Returns PASS or FAIL with reasoning.
author: Lewis James Burgess

decision_description.description: A plain-language description of the decision or action to be evaluated. Should include: what the action is, who it affects, and the context in which it is being taken.

individual_affected.description: Whether the decision affects an identifiable individual person.

result.description: PASS if the individual was considered as an individual human being. FAIL if they were processed as a unit within a system. NOT_APPLICABLE if no individual is affected.

reasoning.description: Explanation of why the decision passes or fails the binary test.

recommendation.description: If FAIL, guidance on how to modify the approach to pass the test. Empty if PASS or NOT_APPLICABLE.
